issues.body: `needs_user` sets `alert_configured = bool(alert_repo and alert_token)` and only records an alert failure when that value is true. Consequently, either a missing repository or missing token makes `_ops_alert` return false but is treated as an operator opt-out, after which `set_review_state(..., "needs-user")` removes the PR from sweep enumeration. This recreates the core stranded-escalation outcome the transaction is intended to prevent: the PR can be parked with no confirmed human page. The new self-test explicitly requires this permissive behavior. Trust-plane routing must fail closed when the route or credential is missing.
issues.fix_hint: Treat an absent or partially configured alert route as an unconfirmed required mutation: add a failure and do not apply the terminal parking label. Update the self-test to assert that missing `alert_repo` and/or `alert_token` raises and never records a `park` call.
issues.title: Missing alert configuration parks the PR without paging a human

verdict.summary: The reconciliation ordering and verified configured-alert path are materially stronger, and the new tests exercise those paths. However, terminal escalation still parks the PR when the alert route is absent. In this trust plane, a missing route or credential must fail closed; treating missing paging configuration as an opt-out can again exclude the PR from the sweep without paging a human. This is codified by a self-test, so the change is not safe to approve.
